objective: Close F-0170 by making the paginated AI task page the sole history window and loading draft results in one owner-scoped, unpaginated batch by those task public IDs. Preserve existing workspace, owner, generation-kind, role, redaction and formal-adoption boundaries without schema, migration, persistent database execution, Provider calls or entitlement expansion.
blockedFiles: schema, migration or persistent database execution
new role, entitlement, authorization scope, quota or default capability
raw AI input, output, provider payload or unredacted generated content
Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApiCallers: Content and organization AI history UIs consume generatedResult only from the existing admin history GET response; no UI change is needed.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: admin-ai-generation-local-contract-route currently applies the same page and offset independently to the all-status task set and the draft-result subset, then joins only those two windows.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: The result repository and Postgres gateway need one owner/workspace/generation-kind scoped batch read by the current task page public IDs, without a second pagination window.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: Existing ai_generation_task and admin_ai_generation_result task_public_id relationship is sufficient. No schema or migration source is needed.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: Route tests cover mixed failed, pending, cancelled and succeeded tasks across a page boundary, late results, reversed completion order and empty pages. Repository tests cover exact task-ID, owner, workspace, generation-kind and draft-status scope in one batch.
authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: SC-BACKEND-LIST-CONTRACT and AI closed-loop traceability require task rows, associated results and total to describe one stable task result set. ADR-002 keeps database access in the repository and ADR-007 preserves existing role authorization.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: No F-0146 snapshot, F-0154 metadata, UI, schema, migration, real database, Provider, dependency, permission, deployment or mechanism change.
